_comment: Version-bump recipe for /bump and /release (see ~/.claude/skills/bump). Source of truth is src/finbreak/__init__.py __version__. 'files' are mechanical exact-string edits; the CHANGELOG entry cuts the accumulated [Unreleased] block into a dated section (bodies are authored during dev via changelog_log, so no todo is needed). post_check asserts every version-bearing file agrees. README.md's 'Current version' line is bumped mechanically (files) and drift-gated (post_check); its feature/'what's new' prose is refreshed via a todo at bump time so it stays layman-friendly (user directive 2026-07-12). NOTE: this recipe covers the version bump only -- the signed AppImage build + publish is a separate manual step (scripts/build-release-appimage.sh, verify the .sig against RELEASE_PUBLIC_KEY_B64, then gh release create v<NEW> with the AppImage + .sig attached).
post_check: V=$(sed -n 's/^__version__ = "\([0-9.]*\)"/\1/p' src/finbreak/__init__.py); test -n "$V" && grep -q "^version = \"$V\"$" pyproject.toml && grep -q "__version__ == \"$V\"" tests/test_smoke.py && grep -q "^## \[$V\] - " CHANGELOG.md && grep -qF "Current version: **$V**" README.md && echo "version lockstep OK: $V" || { echo "VERSION DRIFT: expected $V across __init__.py, pyproject.toml, tests/test_smoke.py, README.md, and a dated CHANGELOG section"; exit 1; }
todos: Refresh README.md for v{NEW} so it stays LAYMAN-FRIENDLY (plain language, no jargon): update the feature list / 'what's new' wording to reflect the user-visible changes in the new [{NEW}] CHANGELOG section. The 'Current version' line is bumped mechanically by the files step (so just confirm it reads {NEW}); this todo is only the prose. Keep it short and outcome-focused -- what the app now does for the user, not how it works internally.
